title: OpenAI Rolls Out ‘Advanced’ Security Mode for At-Risk Accounts
description: OpenAI is rolling out Advanced Account Security for people concerned that their ChatGPT or Codex accounts could be potential targets of phishing attacks.

title: 90,000 Screenshots of One Celebrity's Phone Were Exposed Online
description: Extremely sensitive personal data from a European celebrity that appears to have been compiled using spyware was publicly accessible until a researcher flagged the exposure.

title: Why Sharing a Screenshot Can Get You Jailed in the UAE | WIRED
description: The war in Iran has drawn attention to arrests in the United Arab Emirates over online content, but the legal framework behind that enforcement has existed for years.

title: The Race Is on to Keep AI Agents From Running Wild With Your Credit Cards | WIRED
description: AI agents may soon be buying your stuff for you. The FIDO Alliance has teamed up with Google and Mastercard to try to ensure that shopping in the near future isn't a complete disaster.

title: Cole Allen Charged With Attempting to Assassinate Trump | WIRED
description: The suspected shooter at Saturday night’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner faces three felony charges. He remains in custody following Monday’s hearing.

title: California Engineer Identified in Suspected Shooting at White House Correspondents’ Dinner | WIRED
description: A 31-year-old engineer and self-described indie game developer is suspected of firing shots at the annual event attended by President Donald Trump, high-profile media figures, and US government officials.

title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos | WIRED
description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

title: The Latest Push to Extend Key US Spy Powers Is Still a Mess | WIRED
description: A US surveillance program that lets the FBI view Americans’ communications without a warrant is up for renewal. A new bill aims to address mounting lawmaker concerns—with smoke and mirrors.

title: Newly Deciphered Sabotage Malware May Have Targeted Iran’s Nuclear Program—and Predates Stuxnet | WIRED
description: Researchers have finally cracked Fast16, mysterious code capable of silently tampering with calculation and simulation software. It was created in 2005—and likely deployed by the US or an ally.
